title: SketchLines Object
description: The collection of lines in a sketch. This provides access to the existing lines and supports the methods to create new lines.

methods.name: addAngleChamfer
methods.description: Creates a chamfer between two sketch lines. In the case where the two input lines cross each other creating an "X" shape, this results in four quadrants where the chamfer can be placed. The point arguments are used to define which of the four quadrants the chamfer should be created in. The two points define which side of the two lines should be kept and the other end will be trimmed by the chamfer. The easiest way to use this is to use the end points of the lines on the side you want to keep. In the case where the lines don't intersect or connect at the end points, there is only one valid quadrant for the chamfer so the points are ignored.

methods.name: addByMidpoint
methods.description: Creates a sketch line where the first point is the midpoint and the second point is one endpoint. The system automatically calculates the other endpoint to create a line where the first point is exactly at the midpoint.

methods.name: addByTwoPoints
methods.description: Creates a sketch line between the two input points. The input points can be either existing SketchPoints or Point3D objects. If a SketchPoint is used the new line will be based on that sketch point and update if the sketch point is modified.

methods.name: addCenterPointRectangle
methods.description: Creates four sketch lines representing a rectangle where the first point represents the center of the rectangle. The second point is the corner of the rectangle and can be either an existing SketchPoint or Point3D object. The four sketch lines are returned.

methods.name: addDistanceChamfer
methods.description: Creates a chamfer between two sketch lines. In the case where the two input lines cross each other creating an "X" shape, this results in four quadrants where the chamfer can be placed. The point arguments are used to define which of the four quadrants the chamfer should be created in. The two points define which side of the two lines should be kept and the other end will be trimmed by the chamfer. The easiest way to use this is to use the end points of the lines on the side you want to keep. In the case where the lines don't intersect or connect at the end points, there is only one valid quadrant for the chamfer so the points are ignored.

methods.name: addEdgePolygon
methods.description: Creates a polygon where two points specify an edge of the polygon. By specifying an edge, the size and position of the polygon is also defined.

methods.name: addScribedPolygon
methods.description: Creates either an inscribed or circumscribed n-sided polygon.

methods.name: addThreePointRectangle
methods.description: Creates four sketch lines representing a rectangle where the first two points are the base corners of the rectangle and the third point defines the height.

methods.name: addTwoPointRectangle
methods.description: Creates four sketch lines representing a rectangle where the two points are the opposing corners of the rectangle. The input points can be either existing SketchPoints or Point3D objects. If a SketchPoint is used the new lines will be based on that sketch point and update if the sketch point is modified.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified sketch line using an index into the collection.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of lines in the sketch.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: API Sample that demonstrates creating sketch lines in various ways.
related_samples.description: Demonstrates several ways to create sketch lines, including as the result of creating a rectangle.

related_samples.name: Simple Revolve Feature Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new revolve feature, resulting in a new component.

addAngleChamfer.title: SketchLines.addAngleChamfer Method
addAngleChamfer.description: Creates a chamfer between two sketch lines. In the case where the two input lines cross each other creating an "X" shape, this results in four quadrants where the chamfer can be placed. The point arguments are used to define which of the four quadrants the chamfer should be created in. The two points define which side of the two lines should be kept and the other end will be trimmed by the chamfer. The easiest way to use this is to use the end points of the lines on the side you want to keep. In the case where the lines don't intersect or connect at the end points, there is only one valid quadrant for the chamfer so the points are ignored.

syntax.python: returnValue = sketchLines_var.addAngleChamfer(firstLine, firstLinePoint, secondLine, secondLinePoint, distance, angle)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = sketchLines_var->addAngleChamfer(firstLine, firstLinePoint, secondLine, secondLinePoint, distance, angle);

addAngleChamfer.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchLine object that represents the chamfer or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: firstLine
parameters.description: The first line you want to chamfer.

parameters.name: firstLinePoint
parameters.description: A point on the first line that is on the side of the intersection with the second line that you want to keep.

parameters.name: secondLine
parameters.description: The second line you want to chamfer.

parameters.name: secondLinePoint
parameters.description: A point on the second line that is on the side of the intersection with the first line that you want to keep.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: Defines the distance of the start point of the chamfer from the intersection point of the two lines along the first line. The distance is defined in centimeters.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: Defines the angle of the chamfer as measured from the first line. The angle is defined in radians.

related_samples.name: Sketch Chamfer API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new sketch point.

addByMidpoint.title: SketchLines.addByMidpoint Method
addByMidpoint.description: Creates a sketch line where the first point is the midpoint and the second point is one endpoint. The system automatically calculates the other endpoint to create a line where the first point is exactly at the midpoint.
addByMidpoint.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchLine object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: midPoint
parameters.description: The midpoint of the line. It can be a SketchPoint or Point3D object.

parameters.name: secondPoint
parameters.description: One endpoint of the line. It can be a SketchPoint or Point3D object. The other endpoint will be calculated automatically.

addByTwoPoints.title: SketchLines.addByTwoPoints Method
addByTwoPoints.description: Creates a sketch line between the two input points. The input points can be either existing SketchPoints or Point3D objects. If a SketchPoint is used the new line will be based on that sketch point and update if the sketch point is modified.
addByTwoPoints.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchLine object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: startPoint
parameters.description: The start point of the line. It can be a SketchPoint or Point3D object.

parameters.name: endPoint
parameters.description: The end point of the line. It can be a SketchPoint or Point3D object.

related_samples.name: API Sample that demonstrates creating sketch lines in various ways.
related_samples.description: Demonstrates several ways to create sketch lines, including as the result of creating a rectangle.

related_samples.name: Simple Revolve Feature Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new revolve feature, resulting in a new component.

addCenterPointRectangle.title: SketchLines.addCenterPointRectangle Method
addCenterPointRectangle.description: Creates four sketch lines representing a rectangle where the first point represents the center of the rectangle. The second point is the corner of the rectangle and can be either an existing SketchPoint or Point3D object. The four sketch lines are returned.
addCenterPointRectangle.return_description: Returns the four new sketch lines or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: centerPoint
parameters.description: The center point of the rectangle

parameters.name: cornerPoint
parameters.description: The corner of the rectangle. It can be a SketchPoint or Point3D object.

related_samples.name: API Sample that demonstrates creating sketch lines in various ways.
related_samples.description: Demonstrates several ways to create sketch lines, including as the result of creating a rectangle.

addDistanceChamfer.title: SketchLines.addDistanceChamfer Method
addDistanceChamfer.description: Creates a chamfer between two sketch lines. In the case where the two input lines cross each other creating an "X" shape, this results in four quadrants where the chamfer can be placed. The point arguments are used to define which of the four quadrants the chamfer should be created in. The two points define which side of the two lines should be kept and the other end will be trimmed by the chamfer. The easiest way to use this is to use the end points of the lines on the side you want to keep. In the case where the lines don't intersect or connect at the end points, there is only one valid quadrant for the chamfer so the points are ignored.

syntax.python: returnValue = sketchLines_var.addDistanceChamfer(firstLine, firstLinePoint, secondLine, secondLinePoint, distanceOne, distanceTwo)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = sketchLines_var->addDistanceChamfer(firstLine, firstLinePoint, secondLine, secondLinePoint, distanceOne, distanceTwo);

addDistanceChamfer.return_description: Returns the newly created SketchLine object that represents the chamfer or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: firstLine
parameters.description: The first line you want to chamfer.

parameters.name: firstLinePoint
parameters.description: A point on the first line that is on the side of the intersection with the second line that you want to keep.

parameters.name: secondLine
parameters.description: The second line you want to chamfer.

parameters.name: secondLinePoint
parameters.description: A point on the second line that is on the side of the intersection with the first line that you want to keep.

parameters.name: distanceOne
parameters.description: Defines the distance of the start point of the chamfer line from the intersection point of the two lines along the first line. The distance is defined in centimeters.

parameters.name: distanceTwo
parameters.description: Defines the distance of the start point of the chamfer line from the intersection point of the two lines along the second line. The distance is defined in centimeters.

related_samples.name: Sketch Chamfer API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new sketch point.

addEdgePolygon.title: SketchLines.addEdgePolygon Method
addEdgePolygon.description: Creates a polygon where two points specify an edge of the polygon. By specifying an edge, the size and position of the polygon is also defined.
addEdgePolygon.return_description: Returns a list of the sketch lines that were created to represent the polygon or null in the case of bad input.

parameters.name: pointOne
parameters.description: The first point of the edge.

parameters.name: pointTwo
parameters.description: The second point of the edge.

parameters.name: isRight
parameters.description: After defining points one and two, a polygon can be created on either side of the line defined by the two points. This argument specifies which side of the line the polygon will be created on. If this is true, the polygon will be created to the right of the line from the perspective of looking from point one to point two. If false, it will be to the left of the line.

parameters.name: edgeCount
parameters.description: The number of edges in the resulting polygon.

addScribedPolygon.title: SketchLines.addScribedPolygon Method
addScribedPolygon.description: Creates either an inscribed or circumscribed n-sided polygon.
addScribedPolygon.return_description: Returns a list of the sketch lines that were created to represent the polygon or null in the case of bad input.

parameters.name: centerPoint
parameters.description: Either an existing SketchPoint or a Point3D object that defines the center point of the polygon. If a SketchPoint object is provided the point will continue to control the center of the polygon.

parameters.name: edgeCount
parameters.description: The number of edges in the resulting polygon.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: Controls the rotation of the polygon around the center point. For a circumscribed polygon, this defines where the center of one of the edges will be positioned. For an inscribed polygon, this defines where one of the corners of the polygon will be positioned.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The radius of the circle in centimeters that the polygon goes to, either outside (circumscribed) or inside (inscribed) the circle.

parameters.name: isInscribed
parameters.description: Specifies if a circumscribed or inscribed polygon should be created.

addThreePointRectangle.title: SketchLines.addThreePointRectangle Method
addThreePointRectangle.description: Creates four sketch lines representing a rectangle where the first two points are the base corners of the rectangle and the third point defines the height.
addThreePointRectangle.return_description: Returns the four new sketch lines or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: pointOne
parameters.description: The first corner of the rectangle. It can be a SketchPoint or Point3D object.

parameters.name: pointTwo
parameters.description: The first corner of the rectangle. It can be a SketchPoint or Point3D object.

parameters.name: pointThree
parameters.description: The first corner of the rectangle. a Point3D object defining the height of the rectangle.

related_samples.name: API Sample that demonstrates creating sketch lines in various ways.
related_samples.description: Demonstrates several ways to create sketch lines, including as the result of creating a rectangle.

addTwoPointRectangle.title: SketchLines.addTwoPointRectangle Method
addTwoPointRectangle.description: Creates four sketch lines representing a rectangle where the two points are the opposing corners of the rectangle. The input points can be either existing SketchPoints or Point3D objects. If a SketchPoint is used the new lines will be based on that sketch point and update if the sketch point is modified.
addTwoPointRectangle.return_description: Returns the four new sketch lines or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: pointOne
parameters.description: The first corner of the rectangle. It can be a SketchPoint or Point3D object.

parameters.name: pointTwo
parameters.description: The second corner of the rectangle. It can be a SketchPoint or Point3D object.

related_samples.name: API Sample that demonstrates creating sketch lines in various ways.
related_samples.description: Demonstrates several ways to create sketch lines, including as the result of creating a rectangle.

related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new sketch point.

classType.title: SketchLines.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: SketchLines.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified sketch line using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

count.title: SketchLines.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of lines in the sketch.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchLines_var.count

isValid.title: SketchLines.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchLines_var.isValid

objectType.title: SketchLines.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sketchLines_var.objectType
